meaning: Schema version for the normalized trace format
example_policy: All policies must know which schema semantics apply
source_or_justification: Needed to make C1/C2/versioned adapters interpret fields consistently

meaning: Ordered collection of normalized events in the trace
example_policy: All policies over trace behavior
source_or_justification: Needed because C2 checks policies over an event stream, not a single raw record

example_policy: Tool failure must not be reported as success
source_or_justification: Needed to link events and preserve event identity

example_policy: All policies
source_or_justification: Needed to group normalized events into one execution trace

meaning: Execution order index within a trace
example_policy: Approval before external send
source_or_justification: Needed for temporal order and before/after checks

example_policy: Approval must happen before external send
source_or_justification: Needed when temporal order cannot be recovered from step_id alone

example_policy: No untrusted retrieval to destructive tool
source_or_justification: Needed for causal chain reconstruction

example_policy: Approval before action
source_or_justification: Maps raw events to plan/before_action/after_action/state_change/finish hooks

example_policy: No memory write without consent
source_or_justification: Needed to classify agent behavior across frameworks

example_policy: Delete file requires approval
source_or_justification: Needed for allow/deny rules and action-specific predicates

example_policy: Approval before external send
source_or_justification: Needed to detect dangerous effects such as send/delete/execute

example_policy: Access private data requires auth
source_or_justification: Needed for target binding and same-target approval checks

example_policy: Network access only to approved endpoints
source_or_justification: Needed to bind concrete targets such as recipient/path/url/command

example_policy: Tool failure must not be reported as success
source_or_justification: Needed to link observations, errors, and final claims to actual outputs

meaning: Input event/output/source references consumed by event
example_policy: No untrusted retrieval to destructive tool
source_or_justification: Needed for data-flow and causal dependency checks

meaning: Identifier for output produced by the event
example_policy: Final answer grounded in source
source_or_justification: Needed for output-source linkage

meaning: Source IDs or provenance records used by event
example_policy: No untrusted retrieval to destructive tool
source_or_justification: Needed for source tracking and evidence preservation

meaning: Taint/trust/sensitivity evidence for the event or data
example_policy: No untrusted input to destructive tool
source_or_justification: Needed for untrusted data-flow and source-sink rules

example_policy: Approval before external send
source_or_justification: Needed for approval-before-action and same-target approval checks

meaning: Relevant state before action or transition
example_policy: Valid precondition before tool execution
source_or_justification: Needed for Hoare-style precondition rules

meaning: Relevant state after action or transition
example_policy: Tool updates state correctly
source_or_justification: Needed for postcondition and invariant rules

meaning: Execution or policy status of the event
example_policy: Tool failure must not be reported as success
source_or_justification: Needed to detect failed/blocked/rewritten actions

example_policy: Retry on transient failure
source_or_justification: Needed for recovery and intervention rules

meaning: How reversible, compensatable, or risky the action is
example_policy: Block irreversible dangerous action
source_or_justification: Needed for intervention decisions around hard/irreversible actions

example_policy: No privilege escalation
source_or_justification: Needed to know active policy version and allowed action set

example_policy: If evidence missing return UNKNOWN not SAFE
source_or_justification: Needed to route allow/block/retry/replan/ask_user decisions

meaning: Pointer to original raw trace event
example_policy: All policies
source_or_justification: Needed for audit/debug and to trace normalized event back to raw evidence

meaning: Adapter/debug metadata not part of verifier-neutral core
example_policy: Adapter ambiguity tracking
source_or_justification: Needed for non-core extraction notes without polluting verifier fields

example_policy: No untrusted input to exec/sql/send sinks
source_or_justification: Needed to distinguish trusted/untrusted/sensitive/sanitized data

meaning: Source category or source id for tainted data
example_policy: No retrieval-derived prompt injection to tool call
source_or_justification: Needed to localize where unsafe data entered the trace

example_policy: Explain why a value is untrusted
source_or_justification: Needed for auditability and C2 missing/violation explanations

meaning: Whether sanitizer was applied before a sink
example_policy: Untrusted input must not reach eval without sanitizer
source_or_justification: Needed to avoid false violation when data was sanitized

meaning: Whether validator/policy checker accepted the data
example_policy: Untrusted URL must be validated before network request
source_or_justification: Needed to distinguish validated from unchecked data flows

example_policy: Classified data must be declassified before external output
source_or_justification: Needed for information-flow rules that allow controlled release

example_policy: Prompt-derived data must not reach command execution
source_or_justification: Needed to prove or reject source-to-sink reachability

example_policy: Approval before external send
source_or_justification: Needed to check approval presence without requiring every event to be an approval

example_policy: Delete file requires user confirmation
source_or_justification: Needed to distinguish approved/rejected/missing/expired/not_required

example_policy: Approval for one recipient must not approve another recipient
source_or_justification: Needed for same-target binding

meaning: Actor/user/policy authority that approved the action
example_policy: Privileged policy expansion requires authorized approval
source_or_justification: Needed for authority/audit checks

meaning: Event id of the approval record
example_policy: Approval must occur before external send
source_or_justification: Needed to link approval evidence to event order

example_policy: No unapproved policy expansion
source_or_justification: Needed to know which rule set/capability manifest evaluated the event

example_policy: Network access only to approved endpoints
source_or_justification: Needed for allowlist/capability set-membership checks

meaning: Verifier result for this event/rule context
example_policy: If evidence missing return UNKNOWN not SAFE
source_or_justification: Needed to preserve safe/violation/unknown/inconsistent status

example_policy: Block, retry, replan, or ask user after violation/unknown
source_or_justification: Needed to check intervention before continuation

example_policy: Explain why event was blocked or marked unknown
source_or_justification: Needed for auditability and reviewer diagnosis

meaning: Evidence fields missing for a rule check
example_policy: Do not report SAFE when evidence is missing
source_or_justification: Needed for preservation checker output and ablation analysis
